info.name: Equiflow API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Equiflow trading platform.

All requests route through the API Gateway at {{base_url}} (default: http://localhost:8080).

## Quick start
1. Run all 4 requests in **00 - Setup & Auth** top-to-bottom — this logs in every seeded user and saves their tokens automatically.
2. Use any individual endpoint folder or jump straight to **11 - Workflows** for guided multi-step flows.

## Seeded users (password: `password123`)
| User | Role | Ledger |
|---|---|---|
| trader1 | TRADER | $100,000 |
| trader2 | TRADER | $100,000 |
| regulator1 | REGULATOR | none |
| bot-operator1 | BOT_OPERATOR | none |

## Order status lifecycle
PENDING → COMPLIANCE_CHECK → OPEN → FILLED / PARTIALLY_FILLED / CANCELLED / REJECTED / FAILED

variable.description: API Gateway. Change to target a different environment.
Auto-populated by 00 - Setup & Auth / Login trader1
Auto-populated by 00 - Setup & Auth / Login trader2
Auto-populated by 00 - Setup & Auth / Login regulator1
Auto-populated by 00 - Setup & Auth / Login bot-operator1

exec: console.warn('[Equiflow] Tokens not set. Run folder "00 - Setup & Auth" first.');

item.name: 00 - Setup & Auth
item.description: Run these 4 requests in order before using anything else. Each login saves its token to a collection variable so all subsequent requests are pre-authorised.

request.description: No auth required. Confirms the stack is up and market data is seeded. Expect 30 tickers.
Saves token to {{trader1_token}}. trader1 has a $100,000 funded ledger account and can place orders.
Saves token to {{trader2_token}}. trader2 has a $100,000 funded ledger account and can place orders.
Saves token to {{regulator1_token}}. Required for GET /auth/users and market admin endpoints.
Saves token to {{botoperator1_token}}. Required for chaos and market scenario admin endpoints.

item.name: 01 - Auth
item.description: JWT token issuance and validation. Port 8081, routed via gateway at /auth/**.

request.description:
Authenticates a user and returns a signed JWT bearer token. The test script automatically saves the token to the `token` collection variable.

**Roles:** TRADER, REGULATOR, BOT_OPERATOR

**Validation:**
- `username`: required, non-blank
- `password`: required, non-blank
Validates the JWT token from the Authorization header. The collection-level Bearer auth will automatically populate the Authorization header from the `token` variable.
Returns all registered users. Requires REGULATOR role. Use the `regulator1` token to call this endpoint.

Returns the JSON Web Key Set used by other services to verify JWT tokens. No authentication required. Note: because this service uses HMAC-SHA256 (symmetric), no public keys are returned — other services share the JWT_SECRET.

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: 02 - Orders
item.description:
Order submission, status, cancellation, and order book. Port 8082, routed via gateway at /orders/**.

Order lifecycle: PENDING → COMPLIANCE_CHECK → OPEN → FILLED / PARTIALLY_FILLED / CANCELLED / REJECTED / FAILED

Order sides: BUY, SELL
Order types: MARKET (no limitPrice needed), LIMIT (limitPrice required)

request.description:
Submits a new BUY or SELL order. The user identity is derived from the JWT token — no userId field is needed in the body.

**MARKET order:** Omit `limitPrice`. Fills at current market price.
**LIMIT order:** Include `limitPrice`. Fills only when market price meets the limit.

**Validation:**
- `ticker`: required, max 10 chars
- `side`: required — BUY or SELL
- `type`: required — MARKET or LIMIT
- `quantity`: required, positive number
- `limitPrice`: required and positive only for LIMIT orders

The test script automatically saves the returned `id` as the `order_id` collection variable.

response.name: 400 Bad Request — missing required field

request.description: Retrieves a single order by UUID. Only the order's owning user can view it.

response.name: 200 OK — REJECTED order
response.body:
{
  "id": "b2000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000099",
  "userId": "a1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
  "ticker": "AAPL",
  "side": "BUY",
  "type": "MARKET",
  "quantity": 999999,
  "limitPrice": null,
  "filledPrice": null,
  "filledQty": null,
  "status": "REJECTED",
  "sagaId": "c3000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000099",
  "rejectionReason": "Insufficient funds: required $172,350,000.00, available $100,000.00",
  "createdAt": "2024-03-17T17:00:00Z",
  "expiresAt": "2024-03-18T17:00:00Z",
  "updatedAt": "2024-03-17T17:00:01Z"
}

query.description: Filter by order status — PENDING, OPEN, PARTIALLY_FILLED, FILLED, CANCELLED, REJECTED (optional)

request.description:
Returns a paginated list of orders belonging to the authenticated user, sorted by `createdAt` descending.

All filter params are optional and combinable:
- `from` / `to`: ISO 8601 date range on `createdAt` (e.g. `2026-03-01`)
- `status`: one of `PENDING`, `OPEN`, `PARTIALLY_FILLED`, `FILLED`, `CANCELLED`, `REJECTED`
- `ticker`: stock symbol (e.g. `AAPL`)

response.name: 200 OK — no filters
response.body:
{
  "content": [
    {
      "id": "b2000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "userId": "a1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "ticker": "AAPL",
      "side": "BUY",
      "type": "MARKET",
      "quantity": 10,
      "limitPrice": null,
      "filledPrice": 172.35,
      "filledQty": 10,
      "status": "FILLED",
      "sagaId": "c3000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "rejectionReason": null,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-17T17:00:00Z",
      "expiresAt": null,
      "updatedAt": "2026-03-17T17:00:05Z"
    }
  ],
  "pageable": {
    "pageNumber": 0,
    "pageSize": 25,
    "sort": { "sorted": true, "unsorted": false }
  },
  "totalElements": 1,
  "totalPages": 1,
  "last": true,
  "first": true
}

response.name: 200 OK — filtered by status + ticker
response.body:
{
  "content": [
    {
      "id": "b2000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "userId": "a1000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "ticker": "AAPL",
      "side": "BUY",
      "type": "MARKET",
      "quantity": 10,
      "limitPrice": null,
      "filledPrice": 172.35,
      "filledQty": 10,
      "status": "FILLED",
      "sagaId": "c3000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001",
      "rejectionReason": null,
      "createdAt": "2026-03-17T17:00:00Z",
      "expiresAt": null,
      "updatedAt": "2026-03-17T17:00:05Z"
    }
  ],
  "pageable": {
    "pageNumber": 0,
    "pageSize": 25,
    "sort": { "sorted": true, "unsorted": false }
  },
  "totalElements": 1,
  "totalPages": 1,
  "last": true,
  "first": true
}

response.name: 400 Bad Request — invalid date format
response.body:
{
  "error": "Bad Request",
  "message": "Invalid value for 'from'. Expected format: YYYY-MM-DD (e.g. 2026-03-17)",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-18T02:43:14.855050655Z",
  "path": "uri=/orders",
  "service": "order-service"
}

request.description: Cancels a PENDING order. Returns 409 if the order has already progressed past the cancellable state (e.g., already FILLED or in MATCHING).

response.name: 409 Conflict — already filled

request.description:
Internal endpoint used by the Saga Orchestrator to trigger order matching. Under normal operation you do not need to call this manually — the saga calls it automatically after compliance passes.

Calling this directly is useful for debugging a stalled saga.
Returns a snapshot of the current order book for a given ticker symbol, showing grouped bid (BUY) and ask (SELL) price levels.

item.name: 03 - Market Data
item.description: Real-time simulated market price data. Port 8083, routed via gateway at /market/**.

request.description: Returns current price data for all tracked tickers. No authentication required.

Returns current price data for a single ticker. No authentication required.

Common tickers: AAPL, TSLA, GOOGL, MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, META, NFLX, UBER, SPOT

Manually triggers one price tick simulation for the specified ticker. The price will move randomly within the current scenario's volatility parameters. Useful for testing without waiting for the automatic tick schedule.
Returns current status of the market data service including active scenario information.

item.name: 04 - Market Admin (Scenarios)
item.description:
Market scenario control. Port 8083, routed via gateway at /admin/market/**.

Available scenarios:
- `flash_crash` — sudden sharp price drop across all tickers
- `bull_run` — sustained upward price movement
- `bear_market` — sustained downward pressure
- `high_volatility` — large random swings in both directions
- `sector_rotation` — some sectors rise while others fall
- `liquidity_crisis` — thin order book, wide spreads

request.description:
Starts a named market scenario. The scenario will affect all subsequent price ticks until stopped.

**Valid scenario names:** `flash_crash`, `bull_run`, `bear_market`, `high_volatility`, `sector_rotation`, `liquidity_crisis`

The `triggeredBy` value is auto-derived from the JWT token's username.
Stops whichever scenario is currently running. Prices will resume normal random-walk behaviour.
Returns the current state of the scenario engine.

item.name: 05 - Compliance
item.description:
Compliance checks for order validation. Port 8084, routed via gateway at /compliance/**.

Checks performed:
1. **Wash-sale check** — detects if the user recently sold the same ticker they are now buying (within 30 days)
2. **Insufficient-funds check** — verifies `estimatedValue` ≤ `availableCash`

request.description:
Runs compliance checks on an order before it is matched. Normally called by the Saga Orchestrator automatically, but can be called directly for testing.

**Validation:**
- `orderId`: required UUID
- `userId`: required UUID
- `ticker`: required, non-blank
- `side`: required — BUY or SELL
- `quantity`: required, positive
- `estimatedValue`: positive (used for insufficient-funds check)
- `availableCash`: positive (the user's current available cash)

response.name: 200 OK — rejected (insufficient funds)
200 OK — rejected (wash-sale)

request.description: Returns all compliance check records for a given user ID.

item.name: 06 - Ledger
item.description:
Account balances, fund holds, debits, positions, and transaction history. Port 8085, routed via gateway at /ledger/**.

Transaction types: HOLD, RELEASE, DEBIT, CREDIT, SETTLEMENT

request.description: Returns account balance details for a user. `cashOnHold` is the sum of all active holds for pending orders. `availableCash = cashBalance - cashOnHold`.

Reserves funds for a pending order. Called by the Saga Orchestrator after compliance approval. Reduces `availableCash` without reducing `cashBalance`.

**Validation:** `userId`, `orderId`, `amount` (positive) are required.
Releases a previously placed hold, restoring funds to `availableCash`. Called when an order is cancelled or rejected.

Debits funds from the account upon order fill and updates the user's position for the ticker. Called by the Saga Orchestrator after a successful match.

`ticker` and `quantity` are used to update/create the position record.
Returns all ledger transactions for a user. Transaction types: HOLD, RELEASE, DEBIT, CREDIT, SETTLEMENT.
Returns all stock positions held by the user. Each position shows quantity held and average cost basis.

item.name: 07 - Settlement
item.description:
T+1 settlement processing. Port 8086, routed via gateway at /settlement/**.

Settlement statuses: PENDING_SETTLEMENT → SETTLED / FAILED

Settlement runs automatically on a schedule (T+1). Use the admin endpoint to run manually.

request.description: Returns all settlements with status PENDING_SETTLEMENT (awaiting the next settlement run).
Returns all settlements (any status) for a given user.

Creates a new settlement record for a filled order. Called by the Saga Orchestrator after a successful fill. `settlementDate` is automatically set to T+1.

All fields are required.
Manually triggers the settlement batch process. Processes all records where `settlementDate` ≤ today and status is PENDING_SETTLEMENT. Returns count of records settled.

item.name: 08 - Audit
item.description:
Append-only event audit trail. Port 8087, routed via gateway at /audit/**.

All system events (order submitted, compliance checked, saga steps, fills, settlements) are written here as immutable records via Kafka consumers.

request.description: Returns all audit events across the entire system, paginated.
Returns every audit event associated with a specific order, ordered chronologically. Useful for tracing the full lifecycle of a single trade.
Returns all audit events for a given user, paginated.

Returns all events of a specific type, paginated.

Common event types: `ORDER_SUBMITTED`, `COMPLIANCE_APPROVED`, `COMPLIANCE_REJECTED`, `FUNDS_HELD`, `FUNDS_RELEASED`, `ORDER_MATCHED`, `FUNDS_DEBITED`, `SETTLEMENT_CREATED`, `SETTLEMENT_COMPLETED`, `SAGA_COMPLETED`, `SAGA_FAILED`
Returns all audit events that occurred within the specified time window. Both `from` and `to` are required and must be ISO-8601 instants (e.g., `2024-03-17T00:00:00Z`).

item.name: 09 - Saga Orchestrator
item.description:
Distributed saga orchestration for order workflows. Port 8088, routed via gateway at /saga/**.

Saga statuses: STARTED → COMPLETED / COMPENSATING → FAILED

A saga is automatically created for every submitted order and coordinates the steps: compliance → hold → match → debit → settlement.

request.description: Returns all sagas currently in STARTED status (in-flight orders).
Returns a single saga by its UUID, including all saga steps with their individual statuses.

item.name: GET /saga/order/:orderId — Get Saga by Order ID

request.description: Looks up the saga that was created for a given order. Each order has exactly one saga.

Returns all sagas with the given status.

Valid statuses: `STARTED`, `COMPLETED`, `COMPENSATING`, `FAILED`

item.name: 10 - Chaos Engineering (Surge Simulator)
item.description:
Chaos injection for resilience testing. Port 8089, routed via gateway at /admin/chaos/**.

**Modes:**
- `NETWORK_LATENCY` — adds artificial delay to every request processed by the service (latencyMs: 0–30000)
- `DB_FAILURE` — randomly fails database operations at the specified rate (failureRatePercent: 0–100)
- `BOTH` — applies both effects simultaneously

**Important:** Only one chaos session can be active at a time.

request.description:
Starts a chaos injection session.

**Validation:**
- `mode`: required — NETWORK_LATENCY, DB_FAILURE, or BOTH
- `latencyMs`: 0–30000 (milliseconds). Relevant for NETWORK_LATENCY and BOTH modes.
- `failureRatePercent`: 0–100. Relevant for DB_FAILURE and BOTH modes.
- `triggeredBy`: optional, who triggered this session (for audit purposes)
Stops the currently active chaos session and returns the system to normal operation.
Returns the current chaos session status. If no session is active, `active` will be false and other fields will be null/zero.

item.name: 11 - Workflows
item.description: Pre-built multi-step flows. Run 00 - Setup & Auth first. Each flow is self-contained — variables auto-propagate between steps via test scripts.

item.name: Flow A — Single User Trade Lifecycle (trader1)
item.description: Complete BUY order from price check through to settlement. Run top-to-bottom. order_id and saga_id are captured automatically.

request.description: No auth needed. Note the currentPrice — the market BUY in A2 will fill near this value.
Kicks off the saga: PENDING → COMPLIANCE_CHECK → OPEN → FILLED. order_id is auto-saved.
Check status field. Should quickly reach FILLED. If stuck at COMPLIANCE_CHECK or OPEN, the saga is still in flight — wait a moment and retry.

item.name: A4 — View saga for this order (saves saga_id)

request.description: Shows all saga steps with statuses. saga_id is auto-saved for use in A5.

item.name: A5 — Full audit trail for this order

request.description: Chronological list of every event emitted for this order across all services.
cashBalance should be reduced by the fill amount. availableCash should equal cashBalance (hold released after fill).
AAPL should now appear with quantity 10 and averageCost set to the fill price.
The filled order should appear here with status PENDING_SETTLEMENT and settlementDate = T+1.

item.name: Flow B — Two-Trader Concurrent Orders
item.description: trader1 and trader2 each submit orders independently, then you inspect both their accounts. Demonstrates multi-user isolation.

request.description: Uses trader1_token. Both traders start with $100,000.
Uses trader2_token. Runs a separate saga concurrently with B1.

item.name: Flow C — Regulator Oversight
item.description: Regulator inspects system state across all users. Uses regulator1_token throughout.

request.description: REGULATOR role required. Returns all registered users.
In-flight orders. Empty list means all sagas have settled.
Starts a flash crash. All subsequent price ticks will simulate a sharp drop. Stop with C7.

item.name: Flow D — Chaos Engineering Test
item.description: Inject network latency, submit an order while chaos is active, observe behaviour, then stop. Uses botoperator1_token for chaos endpoints.

request.description: All subsequent requests to the surge-simulator will be delayed 2 seconds. Try submitting an order (D2) to see the effect.
Submit while chaos is active. Check the Postman console — response time should reflect the injected latency.
